title: Patch serde note: clarify manual map_entry path vs derive path
description: The Patch<T> doc says fields must carry #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if)] but the existing Patch-bearing structs (ChatPatch, PresenceStatusPatch, ChatContactPatch, SpacePatch, SpaceUpdateMemberInput) are serialized via a manual map_entry() call pattern, not via derive. A contributor adding a new Patch<T> field to one of those structs faces two incompatible models: add skip_serializing_if and rely on Patch::serialize (which returns Err on Keep), or call map_entry() in the impl. The doc comment does not name the existing pattern or say when to use each. It should name the map_entry() pattern as the current convention and explain that the skip_serializing_if note applies only when using derive-based serialization.
close_reason: Fixed in round-2 review: Patch<T> doc now covers both map_entry and derive patterns.
